text: The general solution of \( \cos x - \sin x = 0 \) is
explanation: $$\cos x - \sin x = 0 \implies \tan x = 1 \implies x = n\pi + \dfrac{\pi}{4}, \ n \in \mathbb{Z}.$$
name: Questions Item 1

text: The value of \( x \) in \( \left[0, \dfrac{\pi}{2}\right] \) satisfying the equation \( (\sin x)(\cos x) = \dfrac{1}{4} \) is
explanation: $$\sin x \cos x = \dfrac{1}{4} \implies \sin 2x = \dfrac{1}{2} \implies 2x = 30^\circ \text{ or } 150^\circ \implies x = 15^\circ \text{ or } 75^\circ \implies x = \dfrac{\pi}{12} \text{ (in interval)}.$$
name: Questions Item 2

text: General solution of \( 4\sin^2 x - 4\sin x + 1 = 0 \) is
explanation: $$4\sin^2 x - 4\sin x + 1 = 0 \implies (2\sin x - 1)^2 = 0 \implies \sin x = \dfrac{1}{2} \implies x = n\pi + (-1)^n \dfrac{\pi}{6}, n \in \mathbb{Z}.$$
name: Questions Item 3

text: If \( \sin \alpha = \sin \beta \) and \( \cos \alpha = \cos \beta \), then \( \alpha - \beta = \)
explanation: $$\sin \alpha = \sin \beta \implies 2\sin \dfrac{\alpha - \beta}{2} \cos \dfrac{\alpha + \beta}{2} = 0.$$ $$\cos \alpha = \cos \beta \implies -2\sin \dfrac{\alpha + \beta}{2} \sin \dfrac{\alpha - \beta}{2} = 0.$$ Combining yields \( \alpha - \beta = 2n\pi, n \in \mathbb{Z}.$$
name: Questions Item 4

text: General solution of \( 4\sin^2 x - 4\sin x + 1 = 0 \) is
explanation: $$4\sin^2 x - 4\sin x + 1 = 0 \implies (2\sin x - 1)^2 = 0 \implies \sin x = \dfrac{1}{2} \implies x = n\pi + (-1)^n \dfrac{\pi}{6}, n \in \mathbb{Z}.$$
name: Questions Item 5

text: If \( \sin \alpha = \sin \beta \) and \( \cos \alpha = \cos \beta \), then \( \alpha - \beta = \)
explanation: $$\sin \alpha = \sin \beta \implies 2\sin \dfrac{\alpha - \beta}{2} \cos \dfrac{\alpha + \beta}{2} = 0.$$ $$\cos \alpha = \cos \beta \implies -2\sin \dfrac{\alpha + \beta}{2} \sin \dfrac{\alpha - \beta}{2} = 0.$$ Combining yields \( \alpha - \beta = 2n\pi, n \in \mathbb{Z}.$$
name: Questions Item 6

text: Solve \( \tan x + \sec x = 3 \) for \( x \in [0, 2\pi] \)
explanation: $$\tan x + \sec x = 3 \implies \sec x - \tan x = \dfrac{1}{3}.$$ Solving gives \( \tan x = \dfrac{1}{3} \implies x = \dfrac{\pi}{6} \).
name: Questions Item 7
